raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the passage stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The request implies improving the argumentative chain from prior metric concerns through analysis to the claimed contribution.","R1.3":"Strengthening requires checking that the reported coefficients and ranking changes actually support the claims made from them.","R1.5":"The excerpt may need section-level or paragraph-level narrative reordering to make the setup, method, results, and contribution more forceful.","R1.6":"The most direct responsibility is improving clarity, concision, register, and sentence-level force.","RX.1":"Any strengthening should preserve uncertainty around empirical effects, causal interpretation, and generalization.","RX.2":"The revision must avoid inflating claims beyond what the reported regression and ranking changes support.","RX.3":"The agent should stay within strengthening the paper passage rather than conducting new experiments or rewriting unrelated material.","RX.4":"Because MT metrics can affect system evaluation, the projection includes avoiding misleading claims that could distort downstream benchmarking decisions.","RX.5":"Strengthening should preserve traceability to the stated evidence, named metrics, WMT context, and reported numerical results."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the passage stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, gap, and contribution claim above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The request implies improving the argumentative chain from prior metric concerns through analysis to the claimed contribution.
rationale.R1.3: Strengthening requires checking that the reported coefficients and ranking changes actually support the claims made from them.
rationale.R1.5: The excerpt may need section-level or paragraph-level narrative reordering to make the setup, method, results, and contribution more forceful.
rationale.R1.6: The most direct responsibility is improving clarity, concision, register, and sentence-level force.
rationale.RX.1: Any strengthening should preserve uncertainty around empirical effects, causal interpretation, and generalization.
rationale.RX.2: The revision must avoid inflating claims beyond what the reported regression and ranking changes support.
rationale.RX.3: The agent should stay within strengthening the paper passage rather than conducting new experiments or rewriting unrelated material.
rationale.RX.4: Because MT metrics can affect system evaluation, the projection includes avoiding misleading claims that could distort downstream benchmarking decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Strengthening should preserve traceability to the stated evidence, named metrics, WMT context, and reported numerical results.
